D10 Imaging L5 Evaluate Reference

Lens Distortion & MTF

a straight grid bows out into barrel or pinches into pincushion when the lens misbehaves — and resolution drops as the test pattern gets finer

A lens can warp straight lines into curves. Barrel distortion (positive) pushes points outward — boundaries bulge. Pincushion (negative) pulls them inward. The MTF (modulation transfer function) measures how faithfully the lens reproduces contrast at each spatial frequency; it falls toward zero as test patterns get finer, especially at the edge of the frame.

Push the distortion slider one way for barrel, the other for pincushion. The MTF chart shows centre and edge contrast curves; the edge falls off faster as the frame's geometry breaks.
